published_prior_art.cite: GaoHarshawSavjeWang2026 and LeCam1986: indistinguishable or TV-close observable laws give the standard testing lower bound

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1 is shared-completion ADE LP bounds, not a null-vs-sparse-alternative testing frontier

published_prior_art.cite: LeCam1986 + GaoHarshawSavjeWang2026: TV separation/impossibility is classical; sparse K-support exposure quotient is the proposed specialization
GaoHarshawSavjeWang2026 gives a positive no-interference vs network-linear-in-means case; this proposes a threshold-map sparse-network witness

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_random_set prior fragments cover random-set envelopes and finite endpoint certificates, not this exact testing-to-reporting rule

published_prior_art.cite: BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011 random-set sharp regions; SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026 sharp direct/spillover exposure bounds

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship iff is not positioned against the classical TV-distance testing frontier, e.g. LeCam1986; without a non-classical sparse-exposure contribution beyond finite model-class separation, tier=field below novelty_target=flagship.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The only concrete noncorner witness is not a valid K=2 threshold-exposure instance: interior-unit means differ between (z_L,z_R)=(1,0) and (0,1) although both have the same binary threshold label.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2 / Example 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed response surface depends separately on left and right neighbors, but the stated alternative class uses binary threshold labels; this violates exposure sufficiency for the proposed witness.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: Lambda_K(P;E0)=0 is a model-class distance condition depending only on X(P); it does not imply the observed law P itself lies in a colliding null/alternative fiber with Q0=Q1=P.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the noncorner witness with a K=2-compatible threshold response surface, or raise K and restate the witness class so every same-label assignment cell has the same Bernoulli mean.

State Conjecture 1 against classical TV/Le Cam testing theory explicitly, isolating the new causal object as a computable sparse-exposure quotient or complexity-controlled LP frontier rather than generic finite-set separation.
Repair Conjecture 3 by adding P in Col(E0,E1,K) or by replacing the pointwise P statement with a nearest-colliding-fiber/reporting rule.

recommended_next_step: Revise the witness and Conjecture 3 assumptions, then sharpen Conjecture 1 so the sparse collision quotient carries a genuinely flagship contribution beyond classical TV testing.
